mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.8 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the hazard ratio for negative returns after UNRATE crosses 5.8% is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate profit margins and equity valuations, with the combined effect amplifying single-series signals.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO × HOUST) shows no predictive power for forward returns in out-of-sample testing, or single-series models outperform the joint model in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising inflation expectations erode real yields and compress equity risk premiums, causing negative forward returns that reverse once the tightening cycle pauses.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral during identified tightening phases, or the regime filter fails to isolate periods where inflation and policy rates move inversely to equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating growth in real disposable income fuels consumer expenditure and corporate revenue beats, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS growth rates drops below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or forward returns fail to correlate with income acceleration in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial output reflects robust capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which lowers input cost inflation and supports equity multiples over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly higher when INDPRO meets the trigger condition compared to baseline periods, or the signal produces negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The joint strength of employment growth and industrial production signals a self-reinforcing economic expansion that sustains corporate earnings growth and equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and INDPRO signal fails to predict positive returns in walk-forward testing, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains elevated while unemployment ticks upward, it indicates a policy lag that typically precedes economic slowdown, depressing equity valuations over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 30 days
failure_condition: Equity returns do not decline following the specified rate-unemployment divergence, or the trigger condition yields a positive predictive value below 40% in out-of-sample validation.
